creator: social@brandongrows.me
text:
That's perfect. Poudriere is something I've got set up and have been using for some things. I'm still figuring out how to use it effectively. I've used Portlint some but haven't tried the others yet so those links are welcomed.

For the fatal warnings, does system information matter there? One of the core benefits to the build system that the engine proper uses is that it uses sysroot jails for building the binaries. The jail system is not new to me. I've been a part of this ecosystem for about four years now but only recently wanted to finally contribute back.

About the tinyfiledialog library, the dev also doesn't provide upgrade patches. That's never a fun time. Right now, the only real breakage with Ren'py when it comes to this library is on the Cython end and just patching it to a new version when needed to keep it building is not going to be an issue. This library is barely used for the engine and could probably be just replaced on my end.
